title: Universal Credit
content: Universal Credit provides financial support for people on a low income or out of work. Eligibility depends on income, savings, and personal circumstances.

title: Housing Benefit
content: Housing Benefit may help people on low incomes pay rent. Most new applicants should apply for Universal Credit housing support instead.

title: Council Tax Reduction
content: Council Tax Reduction can lower council tax bills for eligible residents with low income or limited savings.

title: Food Bank Support
content: Food banks provide emergency food parcels for people facing financial hardship and food insecurity.

title: Debt Advice
content: Free debt advice services help people manage debts, negotiate with creditors, and create repayment plans.

title: Mental Health Services
content: NHS mental health services offer support for anxiety, depression, stress, and other mental health conditions.

title: Employment Support
content: Job centres and employment programmes help people find work, improve CVs, and prepare for interviews.

title: Disability Benefits
content: Personal Independence Payment may provide financial support for people with long-term health conditions or disabilities.

title: Domestic Abuse Support
content: Specialist services provide confidential advice, emergency accommodation, and safety planning for people experiencing domestic abuse.

title: Homelessness Assistance
content: Local councils can assess homelessness applications and help eligible people find emergency or longer-term accommodation.
